growth_records.cite: Mahoney et al. Molecular basis of hemoglobin binding and heme removal in Corynebacterium diphtheriae. Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America (2024). DOI:10.1073/pnas.2411833122. [PMC11725911]
growth_records.snippet: we first determined the maximum growth rate (µmax) at saturating Hb levels and the substrate saturation constant (Ks) for WT cells grown in varying concentrations of Hb to be 0.29 ± 0.01 h−1 and 0.56 ± 0.10 µM respective
growth_records.notes: growth_rate_per_h (µmax) = 0.29 ± 0.01 h-1 is directly REPORTED by authors for WT C. diphtheriae strain 1737 grown on varying Hb concentrations as sole iron source in low-iron mPGT medium (FeCl3 0.25 µM). doubling_time_h was CALCULATED as ln2/µmax = ln2/0.29 = 2.39 h. Substrate saturation constant Ks = 0.56 ± 0.10 µM Hb was also reported but is a Monod half-saturation constant, not a flux/uptake rate, so not recorded under uptake_rates. Temperature and pH of this specific growth assay are not given in the main-text XML (may be in SI Appendix, not included in this file); a related hemin-release kinetics experiment elsewhere in the paper used 25°C or 37°C but that is a different measurement (heme release kinetics, not the growth curve).

phenotypes.base_medium: Acid/gas-production test: peptone/complex base + substrate + pH indicator — positive = fermentative acid production, NOT growth on the substrate as a sole carbon source (interpret with care for model validation)
phenotypes.citation: BacDive strain 3153 (Reimer LC et al., BacDive 2022, NAR); https://bacdive.dsmz.de/strain/3153
